# Shoreline

> Shoreline is a podcast about applied AI — agents, company memory, workflow automation, token economics, and how practitioners actually use these tools. This site is a companion library of episode notes and resources. Named for Shoreline Boulevard in Mountain View, where hosts Enzo Sison and Will Meinhardt lived near Google headquarters.

## Episodes

- [Episode 1: AI Agents and Knowledge Management: Building a Company Brain](https://shoreline-pod.com/episodes/episode-1): AI agents and the "company brain" — local models, Notion and CRM workflows, agent lanes, and review loops.
- [Episode 2: AI Workflow Automation for Sales, Prospecting, and Productivity](https://shoreline-pod.com/episodes/episode-2): AI sales workflows, Telegram voice capture, and company-brain operations.
- [Episode 3: Token Maxing and the Company Brain Era](https://shoreline-pod.com/episodes/episode-3): Token economics, model routing, and Codex vs Claude Code vs Cursor.
- [Episode 4: The Expert's Moat and the Beginner's Mind](https://shoreline-pod.com/episodes/episode-4): Building from your phone, the draining expert moat, system engineering, and local models.

## Topics

- [Company brain](https://shoreline-pod.com/topics/company-brain): A guide to building the shared memory that makes AI agents useful inside a business.
- [Token economics](https://shoreline-pod.com/topics/token-economics): Token maxing, model routing, AI spend incentives, and open-source fallbacks.
- [Tool comparisons](https://shoreline-pod.com/topics/tool-comparisons): Codex vs Claude Code vs Cursor, agent harnesses, and how to score them on real work.

## More

- [Home](https://shoreline-pod.com/): Latest episode, topic index, and recent notes.
